during_call.interpretation: DB2 changed to the multi-instance TON's effective context (200) for the call duration

after_call.interpretation: After return from CALL, DB2 should ideally be restored to 173. The snapshot shows DB2 still at 200 because the multi-instance TON's instance is embedded in DB173 — DB2 reflects the inner sub-DB scope. This is normal for IEC multi-instance timers.

key_rlo_transition.explanation: TON's Q output is 0 because the 15-second timer has not yet elapsed. RLO drops to 0; subsequent S #Control.K208_RunTimeout does NOT execute (S only fires on RLO=1).

expected_diagnosis.verdict: Normal. The CALL boundary correctly preserves DB1; DB2 reflects the called sub-instance scope. The Q=0 indicates the timer is still running (less than 15 s since enable went TRUE), which is consistent with the trigger A K208.RUN being TRUE this scan.
patterns_observed: Project standard pattern: enable → store to L 8.0 → BLD 103 → CALL multi-instance TON → check .Q (matches awl-project-style.md)
next_steps: If the engineer wants to verify timer timing, route to awlsim-runner with --virtual-time-ms-per-cycle to advance the simulator clock without sleeping.
If timer never elapses on the live PLC, investigate whether the enable condition stays TRUE for the full 15 s (use snapshot-to-simulation pipeline).
